_meta.keyKnowledge: We explore how movement and dance communicate ideas, drawing on works from many contexts including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander artists.

_source.w: We explore how movement and dance communicate ideas, drawing on works from many contexts including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander artists.
y: Studying dance from diverse sources deepens our understanding of how movement carries meaning.
Analysing different choreographic approaches helps us develop our own artistic voice.
Understanding cultural context helps us appreciate dance beyond just the physical movements.
eg: We explore how dances use choreographic devices such as repetition, unison and contrast.
We investigate culturally specific dance from a country in the Asia-Pacific region.
We analyse how professional choreographers use the elements of dance to express ideas.
We compare dance styles from different cultures and identify similarities and differences.
We explore how contemporary Australian dance draws on diverse cultural influences.
We discuss the cultural protocols involved in learning about dance from different communities.
